identity.short_name: Blow-up limit L*=1; rational-phase trig selector; weighted sum mod 1000

subdomain(s): singular first-order ODE (Riccati-type) and analytic germs
periodic trigonometric selectors reducible to congruences
CRT-style residue intersection and block summation

problem_spec.summary: A singular Riccati ODE with a specified real-analytic germ at 0 blows up in finite positive time. A normalized blow-up limit L* is extracted and inserted into trigonometric inequalities whose phases are rational multiples of \pi, converting the selector to congruence conditions. The resulting weighted sum up to N is computed by one-period residue enumeration and block summation, then reduced mod 1000.
givens: f is real-analytic near 0 with f(0)=0 and f'(0)=1.
Let beta be the right endpoint of maximal existence on (0,beta) (beta is finite).
Define R = sum_{n=1}^{2025} n * 1_{ { cos(pi*n/7 + pi*L_star) > 0 and sin(pi*n/9 - (pi/2)*L_star) < 0 } }.

parameter(s).name: N
parameter(s).purpose: upper limit of the weighted sum (here 2025)

parameter(s).name: trig_denominators
parameter(s).purpose: sets periods 14 and 18, hence joint period 126

parameter(s).name: phase_recipe
parameter(s).purpose: ensures trig sign tests reduce to congruences once L_star is known

invariant(s).name: analytic_uniqueness_via_series_recursion
invariant(s).statement: Writing f(x)=sum a_n x^n with a_0=0,a_1=1 and matching coefficients in x f'=x+f^2 yields a unique recursion for a_n (n>=2), hence a unique analytic germ at 0.

invariant(s).name: finite_positive_blow_up_time
invariant(s).statement: For x>0, f'(x)=1+f(x)^2/x>0 and comparison f' >= f^2/x forces a finite blow-up time beta<infty.

invariant(s).name: normalized_blowup_limit_universal
invariant(s).statement: As x->beta^-, f(x)->+infty and f'/f^2 = 1/x + 1/f^2 -> 1/beta, implying 1/f(x) ~ (beta-x)/beta and therefore L_star = 1.

invariant(s).name: congruence_reduction_of_trig_signs
invariant(s).statement: With phases pi*L_star and (pi/2)*L_star, and with L_star=1, the sign conditions become cos(pi*n/7)<0 and cos(pi*n/9)>0, depending only on n mod 14 and mod 18 respectively.

invariant(s).name: periodic_selector_joint_period
invariant(s).statement: The joint selector is periodic in n with period lcm(14,18)=126, enabling block summation up to N.

essential_assumption(s): The analytic solution through x=0 is selected by f(0)=0,f'(0)=1.
Continuation is valid for x>0 as long as f stays finite.
Strict trig inequalities are used; the chosen phases avoid zero cases for integer n.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The series recursion determines f uniquely near 0; maximal continuation determines beta uniquely. The normalization identity forces L_star=1 uniquely. Substituting L_star fixes the congruence-based selector uniquely on a finite period, so the resulting weighted sum and its residue mod 1000 are unique.

technique(s).name: series_solution_and_recursion
technique(s).purpose: certify local analytic existence/uniqueness and fix the germ at 0

technique(s).name: normalized_blowup_asymptotic
technique(s).purpose: compute L_star without solving for beta explicitly

technique(s).name: trig_signs_to_congruences
technique(s).purpose: convert trig inequalities into residue classes mod 14 and 18

technique(s).name: CRT_and_one_period_enumeration
technique(s).purpose: find the valid residues mod 126 and compute their count and sum

technique(s).name: block_sum_and_mod
technique(s).purpose: compute the weighted sum up to N by full periods plus remainder, then reduce mod 1000

theorem(s).name: analytic_existence_uniqueness_for_ODE
theorem(s).role: supports uniqueness of the analytic germ fixed by recursion

theorem(s).name: ODE_continuation_theorem
theorem(s).role: rules out finite beta with finite f(beta^-)

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem_with_gcd_2
theorem(s).role: solvability and uniqueness mod 126 for congruences mod 14 and 18

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: periodic reduction to finite residue enumeration

critical_trick(s): Extract L_star=1 from universal blow-up normalization.
Choose phases as rational multiples of \pi so trig sign tests become pure congruences.
Use one-period enumeration plus block summation to compute the weighted sum and reduce mod 1000.

library(s).name: mpmath
library(s).reason: optional numerical verification of L_star=1 and trig-sign checks

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: optional enumeration of residues mod 126 and block-sum verification

task(s).name: verify_residue_set
task(s).reason: enumerate n=1..126 and confirm the 32 valid residues

task(s).name: verify_block_sum
task(s).reason: compute R up to N=2025 directly and confirm the closed-form block computation

task(s).name: mod_1000_reduce
task(s).reason: reduce the final integer to its residue mod 1000
